# 0011. Detect generated files by linguist-compatible content markers

- Status: accepted
- Date: 2026-07-12

## Context

ADR 0010 skips generated files based on explicit `.gitattributes`
declarations. In practice, many repositories never declare anything and
instead rely on GitHub linguist's *content-based* detection: Go's
`// Code generated by <tool>. DO NOT EDIT.` convention, or a
`@generated` marker near the top of the file. Such files collapse in
GitHub's PR view but still flow into rinkaku's output — observed on a
real-world PR where an ORM's generated model files dominated the change
graph despite being visibly collapsed on GitHub.

## Decision

Complement attribute-based skipping with a pure content check on the
first few lines of each changed file, applied at the point where file
contents are already being read for parsing. A file is treated as
generated when its first five lines contain either a `@generated`
marker or a line matching the `Code generated … DO NOT EDIT` pattern —
a deliberately small, linguist-compatible subset of markers. Detected
files get the existing `generated` skip reason (recorded in JSON,
hidden from Markdown, per ADR 0010 as amended). `--include-generated`
disables content detection together with attribute-based skipping.
When file contents are unavailable the check simply does not run,
matching ADR 0010's best-effort stance.

## Alternatives

- **Port linguist's full rule set** (hundreds of patterns plus vendored
  path lists): high maintenance, drifts from upstream; the two chosen
  markers cover the dominant conventions (Go tooling, protobuf,
  Facebook-style `@generated`).
- **Keep requiring `.gitattributes`** (status quo): pushes a config
  burden onto every consuming repository and demonstrably fails on real
  repositories that rely on linguist defaults.
- **Filename heuristics** (lockfile lists etc.): already rejected in
  ADR 0010; content markers are declarations by the generating tool
  itself, not guesses from names.

## Consequences

- Generated code following mainstream conventions is excluded out of
  the box, with no per-repository configuration.
- Small false-positive risk when a file's opening lines merely mention
  a marker (e.g. documentation); `--include-generated` is the escape
  hatch and the pattern set can be tightened without a format change.
- The marker list is a subset by design; files linguist detects through
  other rules will still appear until a marker is added here.
- A pure rename (or mode-change-only diff, no hunks) never reaches the
  content check: the pipeline already skips reading such a file's
  content, since it has nothing to extract symbols from either way. A
  renamed generated file therefore keeps appearing under "Other changed
  files" rather than being detected — an accepted gap rather than an
  extra read solely to satisfy this check, consistent with the pipeline's
  existing IO-avoidance for that case.
